skill.name: excel-analysis
skill.description: Analyze Excel spreadsheets with Python examples for reading workbooks, cleaning data, creating pivot tables, exporting reports, and generating charts.
skill.summary: Use pandas, openpyxl, and matplotlib workflows to analyze and report on Excel workbook data.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static command execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and inline code formatting in SKILL.md. Static weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by ordinary text such as the description field and pandas describe output. The remaining risk is low because the examples read and write local Excel, image, and report files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown fenced Python examples and inline code as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines contain documentation examples, not executable shell invocations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown code fences or inline formatting around Python and package names. No shell syntax, subprocess use, or Ruby backtick execution appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings do not identify encryption or hashing code. The cited lines contain front matter description text and a pandas describe example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata text and line 22 prints dataframe statistics. There is no DES, MD5, SHA1, encryption library, or credential handling context.

low_findings.title: Local Spreadsheet File Access
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples that read Excel files and write spreadsheet or image outputs. This is expected for the skill, but users should only process trusted files and choose output paths carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly perform local file reads and writes. The risk is limited because there is no network exfiltration, command execution, or hidden automation.

content.value_statement: Spreadsheet analysis is slow when workbook data needs cleaning, aggregation, and charts. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical pandas workflows for Excel files.
actual_capabilities: Read single-sheet and multi-sheet Excel workbooks with pandas.
Calculate grouped metrics, filters, sorted views, and profit margins.
Create pivot tables from workbook data and export report files.
Clean spreadsheet data by removing duplicates, filling missing values, and converting data types.
Generate charts from Excel data with matplotlib.
Apply basic Excel formatting with openpyxl styles.
limitations: It provides guidance and code examples, not a standalone Excel application.
It does not validate workbook contents for formulas, macros, or malicious files.
Large workbook performance depends on local memory and installed Python packages.
It does not connect to cloud spreadsheet services or external databases.

use_cases.title: Summarize Sales Workbooks
use_cases.description: Aggregate sales by region, product, or customer and export a clean summary workbook.

use_cases.title: Clean Monthly Reports
use_cases.description: Remove duplicate rows, normalize date and amount columns, and prepare data for review.

use_cases.title: Build Visual Spreadsheet Reports
use_cases.description: Create bar charts, pie charts, pivot tables, and formatted workbook outputs from spreadsheet data.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect an Excel File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the excel-analysis skill to inspect my workbook. Show sheet names, column names, row counts, and basic statistics for numeric columns.

prompt_templates.title: Clean Spreadsheet Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the excel-analysis skill to clean this Excel file. Remove duplicates, handle missing values, trim text columns, and explain each change.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Pivot Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the excel-analysis skill to create a pivot table from this sales workbook. Group sales by region and product, then export the result.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Analysis Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the excel-analysis skill to design a repeatable Python workflow for multiple monthly Excel files, merged outputs, charts, and formatted reports.

output_examples.input: Analyze a sales workbook with region, product, revenue, cost, and date columns.
output_examples.output: A workbook summary with row counts, total revenue, profit margin by region, top products, and data quality notes.

output_examples.input: Clean a messy customer spreadsheet before reporting.
output: Blank numeric fields filled with zero where appropriate.
Date and amount columns converted to consistent types.
A cleaned workbook saved for downstream analysis.

output_examples.input: Create visuals from an Excel category and value table.
output_examples.output: A bar chart and pie chart image with clear titles, labels, and layout suitable for a report.

best_practices: Keep original workbooks unchanged and write cleaned results to new filenames.
Confirm column names, data types, and missing value rules before running transformations.
Use trusted local files and review generated reports before sharing them.
anti_patterns: Do not run examples on unknown workbooks that may contain unsafe macros or hidden content.
Do not overwrite source files when testing cleaning or formatting workflows.
Do not assume pandas inferred every date, currency, or identifier column correctly.

faq.question: What file types does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Excel workbook analysis, especially .xlsx files handled with pandas and openpyxl.

faq.question: Can it create pivot tables?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to create pandas pivot tables and save them into Excel report files.

faq.question: Can it generate charts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes matplotlib examples for bar charts and pie charts based on spreadsheet columns.

faq.question: Does it require Python packages?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use pandas, openpyxl, xlrd, xlsxwriter, and matplotlib depending on the task.

faq.question: Will it protect against malicious spreadsheets?
faq.answer: No. Users should only process trusted files and avoid enabling macros from unknown sources.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it for automation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill gives practical patterns that Claude Code and Codex can adapt into local Python workflows.
